---
title: "AI Agents in Economics and Business Research"
subtitle: "Day 2 - Advanced Practice"
author: "Tinbergen Institute"
format:
  revealjs:
    footer: "Tinbergen Institute | Day 2"
---

## {.center}

::: {.r-fit-text}
Design robust agent systems.
:::

## Day 2 goals

- Build multi-step workflows
- Use tools and memory well
- Evaluate reliability and risks

## Agent system design

- Decompose tasks into modules
- Define contracts between agents
- Add checks at boundaries

## Memory patterns

- Short-term for current session
- Long-term for reusable context
- Store only useful summaries

## LangChain and OpenClaw

- Start with minimal pipelines
- Add tools step by step
- Log traces for debugging

## AI in research pipeline

- Ideate testable research ideas
- Draft stimuli and protocols
- Simulate dry-run interviews

## Synthetic respondents

- Useful for early exploration
- Not human replacement evidence
- Always validate against humans

## Reliability and evaluation

- Test with adversarial cases
- Track failure categories weekly
- Keep a rollback plan

## Sustainability and cognition

- Match model to task
- Limit unnecessary token usage
- Protect deep thinking time

## Task {.center background-color="black"}

**Exercise 5: Build a Mini Agent**

- Define one workflow goal
- Add one external tool
- Submit trace and output

*Groups of 3. 20 minutes.*

## Task {.center background-color="black"}

**Exercise 6: Research Integration**

- Pick one live study step
- Insert agent support safely
- Submit risks and safeguards

*Groups of 3. 15 minutes.*

## Close

- Small reliable systems win
- Humans keep final judgment
- Build, test, iterate, document
